diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with facial swelling and voice changes, with examination revealing prominent edema of the uvula and facial regions, suggesting angioedema. The swelling improved with treatment involving steroids and antihistamines. The patient's medication history indicated a potential link to angioedema due to lisinopril, an ACE-inhibitor. Additionally, an acute kidney injury was noted, possibly related to a recent strep throat infection.
